_meta: Mutation type taxonomy with sampling weights. AVO planner samples a strategy per cycle, biasing exploration across all six categories rather than defaulting to defensive error-handling. Weights adjust based on success rates per strategy.

defensive.description: Error handling, validation wrappers, retry logic, timeouts, circuit breakers, defensive null checks.
defensive.guidance: Use when there are recurring TRANSIENT errors. Avoid when the underlying API is already retried at a lower layer.

refactoring.description: Simplify, extract function, dedupe, consolidate. No behavior change — just structural improvement.
refactoring.guidance: Choose when the same logic appears in 3+ places. Verify no behavior change with differential testing.

capability.description: Add a new tool, agent skill, endpoint, or external integration.
capability.guidance: Use when the system repeatedly fails because it lacks a specific capability. Check capability inventory first.

architectural.description: Change agent hierarchy, context flow, delegation rules, or evaluation pipeline structure.
examples: add reflection step between research and writing
split coder into design and implementation phases
architectural.guidance: Highest leverage but highest risk. Requires cross-file mutations. Use when problems span agent boundaries.

removal.description: Delete unused code, prune dead paths, simplify by subtraction.
removal.guidance: Counterintuitive but powerful. Use when the system has accumulated cruft. Requires self_model dependency check first.

optimization.description: Caching, batching, parallelism, latency reduction, cost reduction.
optimization.guidance: Use when response_time or cost metrics show degradation. Verify with differential testing.
